trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data

regimens.notes: Reasonable for patients with ECOG 0-2. For pancreatobiliary/mixed type ampullary adenocarcinoma. Neoadjuvant regimens vary with regard to use of chemotherapy and/or radiation. Participation in a clinical trial is encouraged [2, 105-110].

trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data

regimens.notes: For pancreatobiliary/mixed type ampullary adenocarcinoma. If considering chemoradiation due to positive margins, chemotherapy should be given prior to the administration of chemoradiation [2, 105-110].

For pancreatobiliary/mixed type ampullary adenocarcinoma. Added by the Panel based on clinical experience. There are no prospective randomized phase III data supporting these recommendations [2, 105-110].

For pancreatobiliary/mixed type ampullary adenocarcinoma. If considering chemoradiation due to positive margins, chemotherapy should be given prior to the administration of chemoradiation [2, 105-110].

trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from biliary tract cancer data

regimens.notes: For pancreatobiliary/mixed type ampullary adenocarcinoma. Based on data from a phase II randomized trial in advanced biliary tract cancer. Median OS and PFS were significantly longer in the combination group versus gemcitabine alone (OS, 11.7 vs. 8.1 months; P < .001 and PFS, 8.0 vs. 5.0 months; P < .001) [9, 129].

trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from biliary tract cancer data

regimens.notes: For pancreatobiliary/mixed type ampullary adenocarcinoma. If considering chemoradiation due to positive margins, chemotherapy should be given prior to the administration of chemoradiation [9, 129].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data

regimens.notes: Should be limited to those with ECOG 0-1. For both pancreatobiliary/mixed and intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. Due to the high toxicity of this regimen, bolus fluorouracil is often omitted [2, 18, 143].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Should be limited to those with ECOG 0-1. For both pancreatobiliary/mixed and intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. If considering chemoradiation due to positive margins, chemotherapy should be given prior to the administration of chemoradiation [2, 18, 143].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data

regimens.notes: Should be limited to those with ECOG 0-1. For both pancreatobiliary/mixed and intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. Due to the high toxicity of this regimen, bolus fluorouracil is often omitted [2, 19, 144].

trial_data.trial_name: Based on data from a phase II trial in small bowel and ampullary cancers

regimens.notes: For intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. The overall response rate was 50%, and the median OS was 20.4 months [11, 132].

trial_data.trial_name: Based on data from a phase II trial in small bowel and ampullary cancers

regimens.notes: For intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. If considering chemoradiation due to positive margins, chemotherapy should be given prior to the administration of chemoradiation [11, 132].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from colon cancer data

regimens.notes: For intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. Derived from trials testing these agents in colon cancer [3, 133].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from colon cancer data

regimens.notes: For intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. If considering chemoradiation due to positive margins, chemotherapy should be given prior to the administration of chemoradiation [3, 133].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: Category 1 evidence for both pancreatobiliary/mixed and intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. In the ESPAC-3 trial, median survival for 5-FU/leucovorin was 23.0 months and for gemcitabine was 23.6 months (P not significant) [5].

Category 1 evidence for pancreatobiliary/mixed type ampullary adenocarcinoma. In the ESPAC-3 trial, median survival for gemcitabine was 23.6 months and for 5-FU/leucovorin was 23.0 months (P not significant) [5, 6].

trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from BILCAP and colon cancer data

regimens.notes: For both pancreatobiliary/mixed and intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. Based on extrapolation of data from BILCAP in biliary tract cancer and from colon cancer [7, 135, 136].

For pancreatobiliary/mixed type ampullary adenocarcinoma. Based on extrapolation of data from ESPAC-4 in pancreatic cancer. Median OS was 28 months in the combination arm vs 25.5 months in the gemcitabine arm (P = .032) [8, 128].

trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from biliary tract cancer data

regimens.notes: For pancreatobiliary/mixed type ampullary adenocarcinoma. Based on extrapolation of data from a phase II randomized trial in advanced biliary tract cancer. Median OS and PFS were significantly longer in the combination group versus gemcitabine alone (OS, 11.7 vs. 8.1 months; P < .001 and PFS, 8.0 vs. 5.0 months; P < .001) [9, 129].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from colon cancer and phase II data

regimens.notes: For both pancreatobiliary/mixed and intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. FOLFOX and CAPEOX are reasonable alternatives. Derived from trials in colon cancer [10, 11, 133, 134].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

trial_data.trial_name: Based on extrapolation from PRODIGE 24/CCTG PA.6

regimens.notes: Should be limited to those with ECOG 0-1. For pancreatobiliary/mixed type ampullary adenocarcinoma. Based on extrapolation of data from the PRODIGE 24/CCTG PA.6 randomized trial in resected pancreatic cancer. Median PFS and median OS were significantly longer in the mFOLFIRINOX group compared to the gemcitabine group (21.6 vs. 12.8 months; P < .001; and 54.4 vs. 35.0 months; P = .003, respectively) [12, 137].
For pancreatobiliary/mixed type, good PS (ECOG 0-1). Increased survival in pancreatic cancer with nab-paclitaxel plus gemcitabine [15, 140].
For pancreatobiliary/mixed type, good PS (ECOG 0-1). Phase III randomized comparison of gemcitabine versus gemcitabine plus capecitabine in patients with advanced pancreatic cancer [16, 141].

trial_data.trial_name: Based on biliary tract cancer data

regimens.notes: For pancreatobiliary/mixed type, good PS (ECOG 0-1). Cisplatin plus gemcitabine versus gemcitabine for biliary tract cancer. Median OS and PFS were significantly longer in the combination group versus the monotherapy group: OS, 11.7 vs. 8.1 months; P < .001 and PFS, 8.0 vs. 5.0 months; P < .001, respectively [9, 129].

trial_data.trial_name: Based on biliary tract cancer data

regimens.notes: For pancreatobiliary/mixed type, good PS (ECOG 0-1) and intermediate PS (ECOG 2). Durvalumab plus Gemcitabine and Cisplatin in Advanced Biliary Tract Cancer [17, 142].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For pancreatobiliary/mixed type, good PS (ECOG 0-1). Due to the high toxicity of this regimen, bolus fluorouracil is often omitted [18, 143].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For pancreatobiliary/mixed type, good PS (ECOG 0-1). Due to the high toxicity of this regimen, bolus fluorouracil is often omitted [19, 144].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For pancreatobiliary/mixed type, good PS (ECOG 0-1) and intermediate PS (ECOG 2). Added by the Panel based on clinical experience.

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For pancreatobiliary/mixed type, good PS (ECOG 0-1). While NCCN recognizes that there is high-level evidence supporting the use of NALIRIFOX over gemcitabine and albumin-bound paclitaxel, it should be recognized that this regimen does not appear to have an advantage over FOLFIRINOX [20, 145].
For intestinal type, good PS (ECOG 0-1) and intermediate PS (ECOG 2). Bevacizumab in combination with oxaliplatin-based chemotherapy as first-line therapy in metastatic colorectal cancer [11, 145].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For intestinal type, good PS (ECOG 0-1) and intermediate PS (ECOG 2). Bevacizumab in combination with oxaliplatin-based chemotherapy as first-line therapy in metastatic colorectal cancer [21, 22, 145].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For intestinal type, good PS (ECOG 0-1) and intermediate PS (ECOG 2). Randomized, controlled trial of irinotecan plus infusional, bolus, or oral fluoropyrimidines in first-line treatment of metastatic colorectal cancer [23, 146].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For intestinal type, good PS (ECOG 0-1) and intermediate PS (ECOG 2). Based on extrapolation from colon cancer data [23, 146].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For intestinal type, good PS (ECOG 0-1). Based on extrapolation from colon cancer data [24, 147].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For intestinal type, good PS (ECOG 0-1) and intermediate PS (ECOG 2). Bevacizumab in combination with oxaliplatin-based chemotherapy as first-line therapy in metastatic colorectal cancer [22, 26, 148].
For both pancreatobiliary/mixed and intestinal type, poor PS (ECOG 3). Capecitabine in patients with advanced or metastatic pancreatic cancer [38, 149].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For both pancreatobiliary/mixed and intestinal type, poor PS (ECOG 3). Oral capecitabine compared with intravenous fluorouracil plus leucovorin in patients with metastatic colorectal cancer [27, 149].
For pancreatobiliary/mixed type, poor PS (ECOG 3). Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data [5, 6].

trial_data.trial_name: Integrated analysis of three phase 1-2 trials

regimens.notes: Useful in certain circumstances for any performance status. Integrated analysis of three phase 1-2 trials. The objective response rate was 57%, and the median PFS and median OS were 11 months and 21 months, respectively [29, 30, 155].

Useful in certain circumstances for any performance status. The overall response rate was 75%, and neither the median duration of response nor the median PFS was reached after a median follow-up of 9.9 months [31, 154].

Useful in certain circumstances for any performance status. FDA issued accelerated approval for repotrectinib for adult and pediatric patients ≥12 years with solid tumors that have NTRK gene fusions [32, 156].

regimens.setting: First-line Metastatic (MSI-H or dMMR, intestinal type only)
regimens.notes: Useful in certain circumstances for good/intermediate PS. Only for intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. In the phase II CheckMate 142 study, the objective response rate was 69%, while 2-year PFS and 2-year OS were 74% and 79%, respectively [33, 153].

Useful in certain circumstances for any performance status. The objective response rate was 34.3%, median PFS was 4.1 months, and median OS was 23.5 months. No patient with ampullary cancer was enrolled in this trial [34, 35, 151].

Useful in certain circumstances for any performance status. Tumour-agnostic efficacy and safety of selpercatinib in patients with RET fusion-positive solid tumours other than lung or thyroid tumours [54, 157].

Useful in certain circumstances for any performance status. In NCI-MATCH Subprotocol H, the response rate was 37.9% (n = 29), with a median duration of response of 25.1 months. In ROAR, in 43 patients with BRAF V600E-mutated biliary tract cancer, the response rate was 47% [36, 37, 158, 159].
For good/intermediate PS. Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data [40, 160].

trial_data.trial_name: Phase 2 trial in advanced pancreatic cancer

regimens.notes: For good/intermediate PS. Phase 2 trial of oxaliplatin plus capecitabine (XELOX) as second-line therapy for patients with advanced pancreatic cancer [41, 161].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For good/intermediate PS. PANCREOX: A randomized phase III study of fluorouracil/leucovorin with or without oxaliplatin for second-line advanced pancreatic cancer in patients who have received gemcitabine-based chemotherapy [42, 163].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For good/intermediate PS. Nanoliposomal irinotecan with fluorouracil and folinic acid in metastatic pancreatic cancer after previous gemcitabine-based therapy [43, 162].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For good/intermediate PS. FOLFIRI regimen in metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma resistant to gemcitabine and platinum-salts [44, 45, 46, 166, 167, 168].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For good/intermediate PS. Second-line therapy after nab-paclitaxel plus gemcitabine or after gemcitabine for patients with metastatic pancreatic cancer [47, 170].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For good/intermediate PS. Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data [19, 144].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

trial_data.trial_name: Based on biliary tract cancer data

regimens.notes: For good/intermediate PS. Second-line FOLFOX chemotherapy versus active symptom control for advanced biliary tract cancer (ABC-06) [48, 175].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 24 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For good/intermediate PS. Best supportive care (BSC) versus oxaliplatin, folinic acid and 5-fluorouracil (OFF) plus BSC in patients for second-line advanced pancreatic cancer [49, 176].
For good/intermediate PS. Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data [15, 140].
For good/intermediate PS. Phase III randomized comparison of gemcitabine versus gemcitabine plus capecitabine in patients with advanced pancreatic cancer [16, 141].
For good/intermediate PS. Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data [5, 6].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For good/intermediate PS. If no prior Irinotecan [44, 45, 46, 166, 167, 168].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For good/intermediate PS. If no prior Irinotecan [43, 162].

trial_data.trial_name: Phase II trial in pancreatic cancer

regimens.notes: Only for known BRCA1/2 mutations. Randomized, multicenter, phase II trial of gemcitabine and cisplatin with or without veliparib in patients with pancreas adenocarcinoma and a germline BRCA/PALB2 mutation [9, 177].
Useful in certain circumstances for any performance status. First-in-Human Phase I/IB Dose-Finding Study of Adagrasib (MRTX849) in Patients With Advanced KRAS G12C Solid Tumors [50, 150].
Useful in certain circumstances for any performance status. KRAS G12C Inhibition with Sotorasib in Advanced Solid Tumors [51, 151].

biomarkers.marker: HER2 positive (IHC3+ or IHC2+ with FISH HER2 amplified)

regimens.notes: Useful in certain circumstances for good PS only. The DESTINY-PanTumor02 trial included a total of 25 patients (all with good PS) with advanced or metastatic pancreatic cancer, with <20% of patients having tumors that expressed IHC 3+ HER2 [53, 179].

drugs.name: Dostarlimab-gxly
drugs.schedule: day 1 every 3 weeks for 6 cycles, then 1000 mg every 6 weeks

regimens.notes: Useful in certain circumstances for any performance status. For patients with recurrent or advanced tumors that have progressed on or following prior treatment and who have no satisfactory alternative treatment options. Patients who had received prior immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy were excluded from the dostarlimab-gxly clinical trial [55, 178].

Useful in certain circumstances for any performance status. If no prior immunotherapy. In the previously treated cohort (n = 119), the objective response rate was 55% and the 1-year PFS and 1-year OS were 71% and 85%, respectively [33, 153].

regimens.setting: Second-line (MSI-H or dMMR, intestinal type only, poor PS)
regimens.notes: Useful in certain circumstances for poor PS. Only for intestinal type ampullary adenocarcinoma. If no prior immunotherapy [39, 153].
Useful in certain circumstances for any performance status. If no prior immunotherapy [34, 35, 151].
For poor PS (ECOG 3). Category 2B. Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data [40, 160].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For poor PS (ECOG 3). Category 2B. Based on extrapolation from colon cancer data [27, 149].
For poor PS (ECOG 3). Based on extrapolation from pancreatic cancer data [5, 6].
For poor PS (ECOG 3). Based on extrapolation from colon cancer data [27, 149].

drugs.name: Fluorouracil
drugs.schedule: continuous infusion over 46 hours every 2 weeks

regimens.notes: For poor PS (ECOG 3). Based on extrapolation from colon cancer data [27, 149].
Preferred for pancreatobiliary, mixed, and intestinal types. Based on data from SCALOP trial [60, 61].
Preferred for pancreatobiliary, mixed, and intestinal types. Based on data from multiple retrospective studies [62, 63].
Other Recommended for pancreatobiliary type only. Based on data from SCALOP trial [60, 61].
